title: Pulp Fiction
logline: The lives of two mob hitmen, a boxer, a gangster and his wife intertwine in four tales of violence and redemption across Los Angeles.
protagonist: Multiple protagonists (Jules Winnfield, Vincent Vega, Butch Coolidge)
antagonistOrConflict: Fate, moral choice, and the consequences of violence in the criminal underworld
tone: Darkly comic, stylized, conversational, violent yet philosophical
themes: The randomness of fate versus divine intervention

actBreaks.description: Vincent delivers Mia home safely after the overdose crisis, completing the first major story thread before the narrative jumps backward in time to Butch's story.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 concludes the Vincent-Mia storyline with their mutual agreement to keep the overdose secret from Marsellus, representing an irreversible pact that fundamentally shifts their relationship and closes the act before the narrative jumps to Butch's story.

actBreaks.description: Butch and Fabienne prepare to escape on the chopper, seemingly free, before the narrative shifts to the final story thread revealing what happened earlier that morning with Jules and Vincent.

validation.ruling: Scene #72 closes Butch's revenge arc as he retrieves Fabienne and prepares to flee on the stolen chopper, marking the final resolution of his storyline before the script transitions back to the diner framing device and Jules's philosophical conclusion.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a triptych with a circular frame—it opens and closes in the same coffee shop diner, but we experience the robbery from two different temporal perspectives. The three main story segments ('Vincent Vega and Marsellus Wallace's Wife,' 'The Gold Watch,' and 'The Bonnie Situation') are presented out of chronological order, creating a mosaic structure where thematic resonance matters more than linear causality. The non-linear construction allows Tarantino to end on Jules's spiritual transformation rather than Vincent's death, reframing the entire narrative as a meditation on choice and redemption rather than inevitable doom. Each segment functions as its own mini-three-act structure while contributing to the larger whole.

The brilliance lies in how the scrambled timeline creates meaning: we see Vincent alive in the diner finale even though chronologically he's already dead, which underscores Jules's decision to walk away from the life that will kill his partner. The structure itself becomes an argument for the possibility of change.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deceptively leisurely, built on extended dialogue scenes that feel like digressions but actually perform essential character work. Scene 8 (the apartment hit) takes 8 pages but only the final 3 contain violence—the bulk is the Royale with Cheese conversation. Scene 29 (Jackrabbit Slim's) runs 6 pages of pure atmosphere and flirtation. The script trusts its conversations. Action, when it erupts, is sudden and shocking (Marvin's death in scene 76, the pawnshop rape in scenes 65-68). The longest continuous sequence is the Bonnie Situation (scenes 73-91, roughly 40 pages), which plays in real-time urgency after the leisurely setup. The final diner standoff (scenes 92-94) brings the circular structure home, replaying the opening but now filtered through Jules's transformation.

incitingIncident.description: Jules and Vincent enter an apartment where three young men are eating hamburgers; Jules interrogates Brett about a briefcase belonging to Marsellus Wallace, then shoots one of the men and continues questioning Brett at gunpoint about the briefcase's location.

validation.ruling: Scene #8 shatters the ordinary world by revealing Jules and Vincent as enforcers confronting Brett and his associates over a business betrayal involving Marsellus Wallace, establishing the main conflict of criminal retribution and introducing irreversible stakes through the power dynamic and implied violence.

midpoint.description: Butch returns to the motel room where Fabienne waits, and they have a playful conversation in baby-talk about pot bellies and body image before falling asleep spooning.

validation.ruling: Scene #52 is intimate character development but lacks structural pivot. Scene #49 is the true midpoint: Butch learns his opponent is dead (false victory becomes devastating revelation), crosses the point of no return from fixed fighter to killer on the run, and shifts from reactive escape to proactive survival with raised personal stakes.

climax.description: Pumpkin collects wallets in a trash bag during a robbery, approaches Jules who refuses to open his briefcase, Pumpkin aims his gun at Jules' face, Jules reveals he has a gun pointed at Pumpkin under the table, Honey Bunny threatens to execute people, Jules takes control and negotiates to keep the briefcase in exchange for giving Pumpkin his wallet cash and allowing the robbers to leave alive.

validation.ruling: Scene #94 delivers peak confrontation as Jules faces Pumpkin at gunpoint over the briefcase, applies his character arc transformation by choosing mercy and philosophical resolution over violence, and resolves the main thematic question about divine intervention and redemption through direct action rather than setup.

scenes.heading: INT. COFFEE SHOP – MORNING
scenes.summary: A young man and young woman sit in a coffee shop debating whether to continue committing robberies, with the man insisting he's done with risky jobs while the woman challenges his resolve.

scenes.heading: INT. '74 CHEVY (MOVING) – MORNING
scenes.summary: Vincent and Jules drive through Hollywood discussing hash bars in Amsterdam and the legality of marijuana in Europe.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEVY (TRUNK) – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules and Vincent open the trunk of their car, retrieve two .45 automatics, load and cock them, and discuss wishing they had shotguns for the job ahead.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING COURTYARD – MORNING
scenes.summary: Vincent and Jules walk through an apartment courtyard discussing Marsellus Wallace's wife Mia, her acting background, and a pilot she starred in.

scenes.heading: INT. RECEPTION AREA (APARTMENT BUILDING) – MORNING
scenes.summary: Vincent and Jules wait for the elevator while Jules tells Vincent about Antwan Rockamora and hints that Marsellus hurt him over something involving Marsellus's wife.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR – MORNING
scenes.summary: In the elevator, Jules reveals that Marsellus had men throw Antwan off a fourth-story balcony for giving Mia a foot massage, leaving him in a wheelchair.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT BUILDING HALLWAY – MORNING
scenes.summary: Walking down the hallway, Vincent and Jules debate whether a foot massage is intimate enough to justify Marsellus's violent reaction, with Jules insisting it's nothing while Vincent argues it crosses a line.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT (ROOM 49) – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules and Vincent enter an apartment where three young men are eating hamburgers; Jules interrogates Brett about a briefcase belonging to Marsellus Wallace, then shoots one of the men and continues questioning Brett at gunpoint about the briefcase's location.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major act of violence — Jules executes someone and threatens the others, establishing the film's brutal criminal world and the stakes of crossing Marsellus Wallace.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) – DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent drives a cherry-red Chevy Malibu convertible with rockabilly music playing on the radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. SALLY LeROY'S – DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent arrives at Sally LeRoy's topless bar with a black briefcase and is greeted by English Dave, who lets him inside.

scenes.heading: INT. SALLY LeROY'S – DAY
scenes.summary: English Dave tells Vincent to wait while Marsellus finishes business with Butch, offers Vincent coffee, and warns him cryptically about taking Mia out, refusing to elaborate on what's funny about it.

scenes.heading: INT. LANCE'S HOUSE (KITCHEN) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: At Lance's house, Jody and Trudi discuss body piercing while Vincent sits at the kitchen table excluded from their conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. LANCE'S BEDROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lance shows Vincent three types of heroin on his bed, explaining their origins and prices, then demonstrates the quality by cooking and injecting the most expensive variety.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARSELLUS WALLACE'S HOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent walks to Marsellus Wallace's house and finds a note on the door from Mia telling him to come inside and make himself a drink.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent enters Marsellus's house and calls out hello.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia sits in the dressing room with video screens in the background as Dusty Springfield plays, and she speaks Vincent's name into a microphone.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent turns around as Mia's voice comes over the intercom telling him she's on the intercom.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia speaks into the microphone directing Vincent to find the intercom on the wall by two African statues.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent walks through the living room following Mia's directions over the intercom until he finds the intercom and says hello into it.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia tells Vincent over the microphone to make himself a drink and that she'll be down shortly.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia's voice over the intercom directs Vincent to the bar by the fireplace, and he acknowledges and licks his lips.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia watches Vincent on a video screen and turns a knob controlling the camera movement while the Dusty Springfield song continues.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent picks up a bottle of scotch, sniffs it, and pours it into a glass.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: A razor blade cuts cocaine on a mirror in the dressing room.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent drinks a glass of scotch in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / DRESSING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia sniffs cocaine in the dressing room.

scenes.heading: INT. MARCELLUS' HOUSE / LIVING ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent sips his drink and looks at a portrait of Mia on the wall, then Mia walks into the room, takes the needle off the record stopping the music, and tells him to go.

scenes.heading: EXT. JACKRABBIT SLIM'S – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent's Malibu pulls up to Jackrabbit Slim's, an elaborate 1950s-themed diner with a giant neon rabbit sign.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKRABBIT SLIM'S – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent and Mia sit in a booth made from a '59 Edsel at the elaborately themed restaurant where their waiter Buddy Holly takes their order, Vincent orders a steak and Mia orders a shake, then they discuss Mia's failed TV pilot and share the five-dollar shake.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKRABBIT SLIM'S (LADIES ROOM) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia snorts a large line of cocaine off the bathroom sink and her head jerks up from the rush.

scenes.heading: INT. JACKRABBIT SLIM'S (DINING AREA) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent eats his steak while Mia returns from the bathroom, they banter about the waiters, then Mia asks Vincent about the rumor that Marsellus threw a man out a window over her, which Vincent confirms was for giving her a foot massage, leading to a dance contest announcement that Mia insists they enter.

scenes.heading: INT. MARSELLUS WALLACE'S HOME – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia and Vincent dance into Marsellus's house singing, finish their dance laughing, then stand face to face in an uncomfortable silence before Mia moves away to get music and drinks while Vincent goes to the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM (MARSELLUS WALLACE'S HOUSE) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent talks to himself in the bathroom mirror about leaving quickly, while in the living room Mia mistakes his heroin for cocaine and snorts a large line, causing her head to jerk back in pain before the rush hits and she collapses.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mia overdoses on Vincent's heroin — a life-threatening crisis that puts Vincent in mortal danger from Marsellus and forces immediate desperate action.

scenes.heading: EXT.VINCENT'S HOT ROD (MOVING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent drives at a hundred miles per hour, speeding through turns and over hills.

scenes.heading: INT.VINCENT'S HOT ROD (MOVING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent drives frantically with one hand on the wheel while glancing at Mia's slack-jawed, unconscious body, then takes out a cellular phone and punches in a number.

scenes.heading: INT. LANCE'S HOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lance sits at home in his bathrobe eating cereal and watching the Three Stooges when the phone rings; he answers and Vincent frantically tells him a woman is overdosing, but Lance refuses to help until Vincent threatens to tell Marsellus Wallace about Lance's drug dealing.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANCE'S HOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent pulls up to Lance's house and begins getting Mia out of the car while Lance yells at him for crashing into his house and talking about drugs on a cellular phone, until Vincent reveals the woman is Marsellus Wallace's wife and threatens to implicate Lance if she dies.

scenes.heading: INT. LANCE'S HOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jody wakes up and walks into the living room to find Vincent and Lance standing over Mia's body on the floor; Lance sends Jody to get the adrenaline shot while Vincent and Jody argue about getting Mia to a hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. SPARE ROOM
scenes.summary: Lance frantically searches a junk-filled spare room for his medical book while Vincent and Jody call out from the living room; Jody enters and criticizes Lance for not keeping the room organized, then finds the book and they rush back to the living room where Lance reads instructions and prepares to inject adrenaline directly into Mia's heart.
scenes.turningPointNote: The adrenaline injection into Mia's heart — the climactic moment of the overdose crisis where her life hangs by a thread and Vincent's fate with Marsellus is determined.

scenes.heading: INT. VINCENT'S MALIBU (MOVING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent drives Mia home in silence, both still shaken from the overdose incident.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT OF MARSELLUS WALLACE'S HOUSE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mia gets out of the car and walks toward her house, then Vincent calls to her and they agree to keep the overdose incident secret from Marsellus, shake hands on it, and Mia tells Vincent her Fox Force Five joke before he leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch Coolidge wakes with a start from a nap in the locker room before his fight, and his trainer Klondike enters telling him it's time and offering encouragement.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY (RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: A taxi is parked in a rainy alley while sportscasters on the radio discuss how quickly Coolidge left the ring and speculate that he knew Willis was dead.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI (PARKED/RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Esmarelda Villalobos sits in her parked taxi drinking coffee while listening to sportscasters discuss the ring death tragedy and its impact on boxing.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOXING AUDITORIUM (RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch throws a gym bag out a third-story window into a dumpster, then leaps down into the dumpster himself, climbs out, removes his robe, and runs to the taxi.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI (PARKED / RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch, soaking wet and wearing only boxing trunks and shoes, jumps into the backseat of Esmarelda's taxi and tells her to get him out of there, and she starts the car and drives off.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOXING AUDITORIUM (RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: The taxi speeds out of the alley and fishtails on the wet pavement in front of the auditorium.

scenes.heading: INT. WILLIS LOCKER ROOM (AUDITORIUM) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: English Dave enters Floyd Ray Willis's locker room where the boxer lies dead on a table with his trainer crying over the body, while Marsellus Wallace stands nearby and Mia and Vincent exchange brief pleasantries.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Floyd Willis is dead establishes that Butch killed his opponent and betrayed Marsellus by not throwing the fight, setting up the central conflict of Butch's storyline.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB (MOVING / RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch removes his boxing gloves and tosses them out the taxi window while Esmarelda asks if he was in the fight and killed the other boxer, which Butch confirms, learning for the first time that his opponent is dead.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHONE BOOTH (RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch talks on a pay phone in the rain, discussing with someone how the fixed fight odds worked out and confirming that his winnings from eight bookies will be collected by tomorrow evening, then tells them to meet him in Knoxville.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL (STOPPED / RAINING) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch pays Esmaralda the taxi fare plus a hundred-dollar tip in exchange for her silence about who her passenger was tonight.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL (ROOM SIX) – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Butch returns to the motel room where Fabienne waits, and they have a playful conversation in baby-talk about pot bellies and body image before falling asleep spooning.

scenes.heading: INT. HONDA (MOVING) – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch drives his Honda while furiously beating the steering wheel because Fabienne forgot his father's watch despite his specific reminder.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREET – DAY
scenes.summary: The Honda races toward its destination as a parking meter's red flag rises.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET CORNER – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch parks his car a couple blocks from his apartment to cautiously check things out before approaching.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch walks down an alley until he reaches another street and discreetly glances out.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET – BUTCH'S APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch surveys the street in front of his apartment building and sees everything appears normal with no suspicious cars or people, then crosses the street and enters the courtyard.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUTCH'S APARTMENT COURTYARD – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch checks the courtyard and finds everything normal, climbs the stairs to apartment 12, listens at the door, and quietly unlocks and opens it.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCH'S APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch enters his untouched apartment, drinks milk from the refrigerator, retrieves his father's watch from the bedside kangaroo statue, and puts Pop Tarts in the toaster.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT COURTYARD – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch walks rapidly across the courtyard, exits the building, crosses the street, goes through the alley, and reaches his car in one continuous shot.

scenes.heading: EXT. HONDA – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch cranks the car into gear and drives away with a wide smile of a survivor breaking across his face.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING STREET – DAY
scenes.summary: The Honda turns down the alley and slowly cruises past Butch's apartment building.

scenes.heading: INT. HONDA – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch laughs and sings along to music while stopped at a light when Marsellus Wallace exits Teriyaki Donut carrying coffee and donuts, crosses directly in front of Butch's car, glances left, sees Butch, and both men freeze in recognition.
scenes.turningPointNote: The hunter and hunted accidentally encounter each other face-to-face, triggering the chase that follows.

scenes.heading: INT. MASON-DIXIE PAWNSHOP – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch races into Maynard's pawnshop followed by Marsellus, punches Marsellus three times knocking him flat, takes his gun, breaks his finger, then Maynard knocks Butch unconscious with a baseball bat and shoots Marsellus with a shotgun.

scenes.heading: INT. PAWNSHOP BACK ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch and Marsellus wake up tied to chairs with ball gags in their mouths in a dungeon-like basement as Maynard sprays them with a fire extinguisher, then Maynard and Zed decide Marsellus will be first and drag him into another room while the Gimp watches Butch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Both enemies become captives together in a horrifying situation that will force an alliance and shift Butch's trajectory.

scenes.heading: INT. PAWNSHOP – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch sneaks to the pawnshop counter, grabs a set of keys, listens to the sounds of Marsellus being assaulted, decides he cannot leave anyone in that situation, and searches for a weapon, finally selecting a katana sword.

scenes.heading: INT. PAWNSHOP BACK ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch quietly sneaks down the stairs to the dungeon while sounds of assault continue behind the closed door.

scenes.heading: INT. RUSSELL'S OLD ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch pushes open the door, sees Zed assaulting Marsellus while Maynard watches, calls out to the hillbilly, slashes Maynard across the front with the sword, skewers him from behind, then points the blade at Zed and orders him to step away from Marsellus.

scenes.heading: EXT. MASON-DIXIE PAWNSHOP – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch exits the pawnshop shaking, sees Zed's chrome chopper parked outside with the name Grace on it, climbs aboard, starts it with the keys, and speeds off.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCH AND FABIENNE'S HOTEL ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Fabienne stands in front of a mirror in a Frankie Says Relax tee-shirt singing along to music from a boom box.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREET – CHOPPER (MOVING) – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch drives the chopper down the street and checks his father's watch which reads 10:30.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOTEL ROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Butch rides up on the chopper and runs inside telling Fabienne to grab her radio and purse because they need to leave immediately, then waits outside while she exits with her belongings and asks about the motorcycle and her crashed Honda.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: The Fourth Man paces in the bathroom clutching his silver Magnum while listening to Jules recite his biblical passage on the other side of the door.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT – DAY
scenes.summary: The Fourth Man bursts out of the bathroom firing six shots from his Magnum at Jules and Vincent, but miraculously misses them all, then Jules and Vincent shoot and kill him.
scenes.turningPointNote: The miraculous survival from point-blank gunfire becomes the divine intervention that transforms Jules and sets his spiritual awakening in motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD APARTMENT BUILDING – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Chevy Nova propels itself into traffic.

scenes.heading: INT. NOVA (MOVING) – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules drives with Vincent in the passenger seat and Marvin in the back, debating whether the missed shots were a miracle or a freak occurrence, with Jules declaring he's retiring and Vincent calling him crazy.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S BATHROOM – DAY
scenes.summary: Jules washes his bloody hands in Jimmie's bathroom while warning Vincent they must be delicate with Jimmie who is one remark away from kicking them out.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S KITCHEN – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules, Vincent, and Jimmie stand in the kitchen drinking coffee when Jimmie angrily tells them to stop complimenting his gourmet coffee and address the dead person in his garage before his wife Bonnie comes home.

scenes.heading: INT. MARSELLUS WALLACE'S DINING ROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: Marsellus sits at his dining table eating breakfast while talking on the phone about how badly Bonnie will freak out when she comes home.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S BEDROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules paces in Jimmie's bedroom on the phone explaining the explosive Bonnie situation and demanding Marsellus solve the problem before she gets home.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SUITE – MORNING
scenes.summary: Winston Wolf sits on a hotel bed in a tuxedo with his back to a crap game, taking notes on the phone about the situation including when Bonnie is due and the principals' names.

scenes.heading: EXT. JIMMIE'S STREET – MORNING
scenes.summary: A silver Porsche whips around the corner at high speed, stops on a dime in front of Jimmie's house, and a ringed finger touches the doorbell.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S HOUSE – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jimmie opens the door to Winston Wolf who introduces himself as a problem solver, then Wolf greets Jules and Vincent and asks how much time they have until Bonnie arrives.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S GARAGE – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Wolf examines the car in Jimmie's garage in silence, sends Jimmie to make coffee, and asks Jules if there's anything he needs to know about the vehicle.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Wolf drinks coffee and lays out a detailed plan instructing Jules and Vincent to put the body in the trunk and clean the car's interior while Jimmie gathers linens to line the car.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S BEDROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jimmie gathers linens while The Wolf talks on the phone about the car and arranges to meet someone named Joe in twenty minutes, then reassures Jimmie he wants to help and they'll get through this.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules picks up pieces of skull and brain in the backseat while Vincent washes windows in the front, both covered in blood, with Jules cursing Vincent for causing this situation.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEVY NOVA – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Wolf examines the cleaned and linen-lined car interior, compliments Jules and Vincent on their work, and tells them phase one is complete but not to celebrate yet.

scenes.heading: EXT. JIMMIE'S BACKYARD – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Wolf orders Jules and Vincent to strip naked in Jimmie's backyard, then hoses them down while they toss their bloody clothes into a trash bag and Jimmie is instructed to burn it.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMIE'S GARAGE – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Wolf slams the trunk closed on the garbage bag and body, then explains the plan to drive to Monster Joe's Truck and Tow with Jules riding with him and Vincent following in the Porsche.

scenes.heading: EXT. MONSTER JOE'S TRUCK AND TOW – MORNING
scenes.summary: Winston tells Jules and Vincent they're cool and it's like it never happened, introduces them to Raquel who jokes about their volleyball outfits, then declines to give them a ride home and drives off with Raquel.

scenes.heading: INT. COFFEE SHOP – MORNING
scenes.summary: Jules and Vincent sit in a coffee shop booth where Vincent eats pancakes while Jules nurses coffee and a muffin, and they debate why Jules doesn't eat pork, discussing filthy animals, personality, and whether a dog has enough personality to override its filthiness.

scenes.heading: INT. COFFEE SHOP BATHROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: Vincent sits on the toilet reading his Modesty Blaise book, oblivious to pandemonium outside.

scenes.heading: INT. COFFEE SHOP – MORNING
scenes.summary: Pumpkin collects wallets in a trash bag during a robbery, approaches Jules who refuses to open his briefcase, Pumpkin aims his gun at Jules' face, Jules reveals he has a gun pointed at Pumpkin under the table, Honey Bunny threatens to execute people, Jules takes control and negotiates to keep the briefcase in exchange for giving Pumpkin his wallet cash and allowing the robbers to leave alive.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jules chooses mercy over violence in a life-or-death standoff, demonstrating his spiritual transformation and completing his character arc from killer to shepherd.
